bullets.text: Iran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, stating that Israel's continued attacks in Lebanon violate an agreement between Tehran and the US to end the conflict.
A global framework for reparatory justice, including a call for formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade, has been adopted at a conference in Ghana.

The CDC plans to allocate $107 million in emergency funding to support the Ebola response efforts in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, as the number of infected individuals surpasses 1,000.
The foreign policy of One Nation is under increased scrutiny as the party gains significant traction in polls, challenging incumbent parties in Australia.
Australian police have addressed descendants at the site of an outback mass killing, expressing regret for the historical events that occurred.
The Minns government plans to address the housing crisis by partnering with the private sector to construct prefabricated housing, signaling a move towards new thinking in home building.
The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge is expected to show faster inflation, reinforcing the consensus for further interest-rate hikes this year.
South Africa's central bank governor warns of early signs of second-round inflation effects and building underlying price pressures, indicating a need for policy action.
A new golden age for Japanese banks is accompanied by a challenge, as smaller lenders are burdened with low-return bonds that they are unable to sell.
